measurements.measurement_reference: Ground floor door ~2.1m, ~28 courses. First-floor windows ~1.3m, ~18 courses.

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard half-lap running bond on all brick surfaces. Upper gable/attic level transitions to siding on one or both units.

colour.description: Medium red-brown brick. Some variation visible with scattered darker bricks. Typical Kensington residential quality. The two units show similar brick suggesting they were built as a pair from the same brick supply.

special_features.description: Simple flat brick lintels above window openings.
Brick rowlock courses forming window sills.
Wooden balconies with railings at second floor level, likely later additions.

condition.notes: 2.5-storey semi-detached pair, brick below with siding in the gable/attic level. Brick in decent condition. Large tree partially obscures left side. White SUV parked in front. Simple residential brick without elaborate decorative features — representative of the more common Kensington building type.

texture_calibration.notes: Tree and parked car partially obstruct facade. Summer photo with full leaf canopy. Coursing visible on exposed portions between obstructions.
